title: You either have freedom of speech or you don't - there's no halfway

transcript.text: Hello, I just thought about doing this video just spontaneously.
I was doing a video that I wanted to do for a long time about how I changed as I got older
And then it kind of at the end it turned into this whole, you know, freedom of speech or not.
So then I'm going to make it the own video because I like talking about it.
And the whole gist of this video is you either have freedom of speech or you don't have freedom of speech.
There's no halfway freedom of speech.
Because if you have halfway freedom of speech, then that means that freedom of speech is defined by whoever has the power, right?
The absolute power to decide who has the freedom of speech.
And then so he draws a little box over here.
But then you're excluding all the people who are outside of that little box, you know?
It's like, oh, that's Catholics.
We get to talk trash about all those people, all those atheists and tell them they're going to burn in hell for all of eternity.
And that the Jews killed Jesus and that they need to get, you know, gassed.
And that the, you know, and all the black people or the animals don't have souls and the black people, their voodoo is going to send them to hell, you know?
So they can't talk about that.
But if you agree with what we say, you can talk about whatever you want as long as you agree with what we, you know, that's not freedom of speech.
You either have freedom of speech or you don't have freedom of speech.
Now, I'm a little bit motivated, I'm kind of, I'm kind of motivated to do this one because, like, my mother, like, I turn on my phone and I have a message.
And it's my mother screaming her head off at me going, I'm so offended by you.
You can't imagine how offended I am about that fuck Jesus Christ video.
So you take that off right now, you know, you're going to, she didn't say that in this particular message, but she said it in a bunch of times before.
You're going to embarrass the family, you know, and I'm not going to allow that.
And you, you know, you come from a good family and you can't do that.
And, you know, you're offending me.
And I've heard other people say that kind of shit, too.
Like, I, like, I mentioned how I, I was thinking about Jesus's hair flowing in the wind and sweaty muscles in the sun.
And some guy, like, is like my, is my, my, my Facebook update.
And some, like, fundamentalist Christian, who I didn't even know was a fundamentalist Christian, who clicked me as a friend.
And, like, goes balls out, balls to the wall on me, like, calls me and yelling at me for an hour and a half, telling me I'm going to burn in hell while I'm returning to all this shit.
And then getting all these other, trying to get all these other people to unfriend me on Facebook.
You know, and, like, going, basically pulling out all stops and going after me.
Frightening to sue me, because I record the conversation, even though he encouraged me to record the conversation and upload on YouTube.
I mean, basically, people getting offended, you know, and it's like, these are my rules about being offended about freedom of speech, okay?
You should, this is, like, how I am.
You should get offended by somebody, or you have the right to get offended by somebody, if they attack you directly.
If they go, if they go scream at you, and if they use, like, emotion, like, if they go, you know, you suck.
I fucking hate you.
You're going to burn in hell.
I think you're an asshole.
I think you're a loser.
Everybody else hates you.
You know, that would offend me if somebody said that.
I mean, or it wouldn't offend me.
It actually would not offend me, but I can understand how it would offend you.
Like, if I did that to somebody, and they said, oh, I'm offended, I would understand that.
I would understand it.
But if I say something that somebody else doesn't agree with, and then they go, oh, my God, I'm so offended by that, then that's their problem, okay?
It's not my problem, and it's not my responsibility to not say it.
Because that's the freedom of speech thing.
Freedom of speech is you either have it or you don't.
So I get to say what I want.
And, I mean, I would say, yeah, it's your right end, from my point of view, responsibility to argue with me what I just said.
Instead of saying I'm offended by it, you go, okay, that's a stupid thing to say, and this is why, you know.
Da-da-da-da-da-da-da-da-da.
And then I would get to go, oh, yay, whee, because that's what's my whistle.
Somebody actually being provocative and going, you know, you shouldn't say that, um, fuck Jesus Christ, because it'll upset this person, and it'll cause these people to not like this.
Then I'd be like, oh, cool, because it'd give me all these reasons to kick your ass in the argument.
And believe me, I would kick your ass in the argument.
That's why nobody's ever going to argue with me about anything.
They're just going to get offended, you know.
But, I mean, that's just me saying I'd kick your ass.
I mean, I'm kind of like, I'm kind of like that.
I mean, I'm not afraid to lose an argument.
I'd love to lose an argument.
Because then, because then I would recognize that I lost the argument, and then I would change my idea, and then it would make me one step smarter, right?
I mean, you don't have to win.
You know, somebody who joins the, joins a sport, they don't expect to win every race, you know, or every, every tournament.
They lose it, and then they get better, and they learn from their mistakes, and then they go back, and they can do better and win.
So then I say something stupid, and put my foot in my mouth, and then somebody, you know, argues with me.
And then I argue back, and then they argue with me, and then they finally get in my head, oh, wait, I was wrong about that, wasn't I?
That's never happened, and it never will happen, but I would be happy if it did, because then, you know what I mean?
It would make me smarter in that way.
But anyways, freedom of speech, you either have it, or you don't have it.
There's no halfway freedom of speech.
That's like the FCC thing that I uploaded, you know?
I mean, people have all these bans of all these words you can't say.
Well, who decides what words you can't say, you know?
It's like illegalizing weed.
Who decides what plant you have to illegalize?
Why not just legalize all the plants that are already in existence since the beginning of time,
And focus on illegalizing drugs that people make that kill people?
You know, like somebody invents a, like I believe in the FDA, that the FDA should test stuff.
I mean, not test something, not have to test something that's natural,
That's made out of natural ingredients, but some kind of like chemical thing,
That if you take one pill, you can die.
Like, that's kind of stuff, yeah, should be regulated,
But like don't regulate like natural things,
And don't regulate natural people saying natural stuff, you know?
So yeah, you either have freedom of speech or you don't.
Like, as far as like Nazis, and then somebody might go,
Oh, but what about the neo-Nazis?

Is don't put laws on what some,
